Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In general, magnitude of creep increases when

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, d)
Creep increases when
• Relative humidity is low
• Temperature is high.
• Loading occurs at an early age.
• Loading sustained over a long period
• Cement content is high
• Water cement ratio is high.
• Aggregate content is low.
• Air entrainment is high.

Q10 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In the design of two-way slab restrained at all edges, torsional reinforcement required is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
For restrained slab, the area of reinforcement in each of the four corner layers shall be threequarters
of the area required for the maximum mid span moment in the slab simply supported on
both edges meeting at that corner. If the corner contained by edges over only one of which the
slab is continuous torsion reinforcement equal to 0.375 times the area of reinforcement provided
at the mid-span in the same direction shall be provided. If both edges are continuous, no torsion
reinforcement shall be provided.

(a, b, d)

Q15 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Match List-I (Post-tensioning system) with List-II (Type of anchorage) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
In the Freyssinet system, anchorage consists of cylinder of good quality concrete and is provided
with corrugations on the outside. It has a central conical hole and is provided with heavy top
reinforcement. In Gifford-Udall system, the wires are stressed and anchored one by one in a separate
cylinder using small wedging grip called Udall grips. Each grip consists of two split cones.
In Lee-McCall system, anchoring of bars is done by screwing special threaded nuts. The nuts bear
against a distribution plate provided at the end of the beam.
In the Magnel Blaton system, the wires are anchored by wedging, two at a time into sandwich
plates. The sandwich plates are provided with two wedge shaped grooves on its two faces. The
wires are taken into each groove and tightened. Then a steel wedge is driven between the tightened
wires to anchor them against the plate.

Q19 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding yield line patterns for slab:
1. Yield lines are straight lines so that they may act as plastic hinges of collapse mechanism.
2. Yield lines terminate at a slab boundary or at the intersection of other yield lines.
3. Each of the segments of the slab will tend to rotate in a rigid body motion.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The following characteristics are useful in selecting the yield line patterns for slab with various
boundary conditions:
• Yield lines are straight lines so that they may act as plastic hinges of collapse mechanism.
• Yield lines terminate at a slab boundary or at the intersection of other yield lines.
• Yield lines act as axes of rotation for the movements of adjoining segments.
• Each of the segments of the slab will tend to rotate in a rigid body motion. The axes of
rotation generally lie along the lines of supports and pass over the columns.
• If an edge is fixed or continuous, a yield line may form along the support.
• For mechanism to develop, yield lines or yield lines produced, pass through the intersection
of the axes of rotation of adjacent slab elements.

Consider the following statements with regard to crack formation and its control:
1. The surface width of the crack should not, in general, exceed 0.3 mm for structures not subjected to aggressive environment.
2. The nominal spacing of main bars in a slab should not exceed three times the effective depth of a solid slab or 300 mm, whichever is smaller.
Which of these statement(s) is (are) CORRECT?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Refer clause 26.3.3(b) and 35.3.2 of IS 465: 2000.

Q25 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(c, d)
As per Annex D-1.8 of IS 456: 2000,
Torsional reinforcement in slab is provided at (and near) corners in a two-way slab which is
simply supported on both edges meeting at the corners. Torsional reinforcement is provided to
prevent the distortion of the slab at corners.
As per Clause 24.1 of IS 456 : 2000, for the slab spanning in two directions, the shorter of the two
spans should be used for calculating the span to effective depth ratio. So, deflection of slab is primarily a function of short span.
During shrinkage, tensile stress and strains are caused due to restraint and hence cracks are developed.
